code: # for this example we will construct a list of genes from the expression data #     to emulate what you might be inputting genelist <- rownames(pset)[which(SummarizedExperiment::assay(pset,                                                      "proteomics")[, 1] > 0.5)]  protdata.enrichment.sets.test <- leapR::leapR(   geneset = ncipid,   enrichment_method = "enrichment_in_sets",   eset = pset,   assay_name = "proteomics",   targets = genelist ) or <- order(protdata.enrichment.sets.test[, "pvalue"]) rmarkdown::paged_table(protdata.enrichment.sets.test[or, cols_to_display]) # in this example we construct some modules from the hierarchical clustering #   of the   data protdata_naf <- SummarizedExperiment::assay(pset, "proteomics")  # hierarchical clustering is not too happy with lots of missing values #    so we'll do a zero fill on this to get the modules protdata_naf[which(is.na(protdata_naf))] <- 0  # construct the hierarchical clustering using the 'wardD' method, which #    seems to give more even sized modules protdata_hc <- hclust(dist(protdata_naf), method = "ward.D2")  # arbitrarily we'll chop the clusters into 5 modules modules <- cutree(protdata_hc, k = 5)  ## sara: created list clusters <- lapply(unique(modules), function(x) names(which(modules == x)))  # modules is a named list of values where each value is a module #         number and the name is the gene name  # To do enrichment for one module (module 1 in this case) do this protdata.enrichment.sets.module_1 <- leapR::leapR(   geneset = ncipid,   enrichment_method = "enrichment_in_sets",   eset = pset,   assay_name = "proteomics",   targets = names(modules[which(modules == 1)]) )  # To do enrichment on all modules and return the list of enrichment results protdata.enrichment.sets.modules <- do.call(rbind,                                              leapR::cluster_enrichment(                                                 eset = pset,                                                 assay_name= 'proteomics',                                                 geneset = ncipid,                                                 clusters = clusters,                                                  sigfilter = 0.25)) ## nothing is enriched rmarkdown::paged_table(protdata.enrichment.sets.modules[, cols_to_display])

code: # This is how you calculate enrichment in a ranked list  # (for example from topology) ### using enrichment_wrapper function protdata.enrichment.order <- leapR::leapR(   geneset = ncipid, "enrichment_in_order",   eset = pset,   method = 'ztest',   assay_name = "proteomics",   primary_columns = shortlist[1] )   or <- order(protdata.enrichment.order[, "pvalue"]) rmarkdown::paged_table(protdata.enrichment.order[or, cols_to_display]) plot_leapr_bar(   protdata.enrichment.order,   title = "Z Test: Ranked Enrichment",   top_n = 12,   fill_sig = "#2166AC",   fill_ns = "#B2DFDB",   wrap = 38 )

title: leapR
text: idea use correlation pathway members versus non-pathway members way assess functional enrichment. idea seems sound- pathways varying correlated way across bunch conditions (say time points patients) may active important others. However, testing validation needed show case.

title: leapR
text: ingroup_mean gives mean correlation pathway members outgroup_mean gives correlation pathway members non-pathway members. Background_mean gives mean correlation non-pathway members. pvalue BH_pvalue pathway members versus pathway members non-pathway components. pvalue_background BH_pvalue_background pathway member correlation relative non-pathway member correlation (similar slightly different p-values).
code: ### using enrichment_wrapper function protdata.enrichment.correlation <- leapR::leapR(   geneset = ncipid,   enrichment_method = "correlation_enrichment",   assay_name = "proteomics",   eset = pset )  or <- order(protdata.enrichment.correlation[, "pvalue"]) rmarkdown::paged_table(head(protdata.enrichment.correlation[or,                                                        cols_to_display])) protdata.enrichment.correlation.short <- leapR::leapR(   geneset = ncipid,   enrichment_method = "correlation_enrichment",   assay_name = "proteomics",   eset = pset[, shortlist] ) or <- order(protdata.enrichment.correlation.short[, "pvalue"]) rmarkdown::paged_table(head(protdata.enrichment.correlation.short[or,                                                          cols_to_display])) protdata.enrichment.correlation.long <- leapR::leapR(   geneset = ncipid,   enrichment_method = "correlation_enrichment",   assay_name = "proteomics",   eset = pset[, longlist] ) or <- order(protdata.enrichment.correlation.long[, "pvalue"]) rmarkdown::paged_table(head(protdata.enrichment.correlation.long[or,                                                              cols_to_display]))

title: leapR
text: example use phosphoproteomics data assess enrichment known kinase substrates (proxy kinase activity)
code: data("kinasesubstrates")  # for an individual tumor calculate the Kinase-Substrate  # Enrichment (similar to KSEA) #     This uses the site-specific phosphorylation data to determine  # which kinases #     might be active by assessing the enrichment of the  # phosphorylation of their known substrates  phosphodata.ksea.order <- leapR::leapR(   geneset = kinasesubstrates,   enrichment_method = "enrichment_in_order",   assay_name = "phosphoproteomics",   eset = phset,   method = 'ztest',    primary_columns = "TCGA-13-1484")  or <- order(phosphodata.ksea.order[, "pvalue"]) rmarkdown::paged_table(phosphodata.ksea.order[or, cols_to_display]) # now do the same thing but use a threshold phosphodata.sets.order <- leapR::leapR(   geneset = kinasesubstrates,   enrichment_method = "enrichment_in_sets",   eset = phset,   assay_name = "phosphoproteomics",   threshold = 0.5,   primary_columns = "TCGA-13-1484" ) or <- order(phosphodata.sets.order[, "pvalue"]) rmarkdown::paged_table(phosphodata.sets.order[or, cols_to_display])

title: cluster_enrichment — cluster_enrichment
code: library(leapR)          # read in the example transcriptomic data         url <- "https://api.figshare.com/v2/file/download/56536214"         tdata <- download.file(url,method='libcurl',destfile='transData.rda')         load('transData.rda')         p <- file.remove("transData.rda")          # read in the pathways         data("ncipid")          # for the example we will limit the number of transcripts considered         #- arbitrarily in this case         transdata <- SummarizedExperiment::assay(tset,'transcriptomics')         transdata[which(is.na(transdata),arr.ind=TRUE)]<-0.0         # perform heirarchical clustering on the  data         transdata.hc <- hclust(dist(transdata), method="ward.D2")          transdata.hc.clusters <- cutree(transdata.hc, k=5)         clust.list <- lapply(seq_len(5), function(x) {            return(names(which(transdata.hc.clusters==x)))})         #calculates enrichment for each of the clusters individually a         #and returns a list of enrichment results         transdata.hc.enrichment <- leapR::cluster_enrichment(eset=tset,                 assay_name='transcriptomics',                 geneset=ncipid,                 clusters=clust.list)

title: leapR: Layered enrichment analysis of pathways R — leapR-package
text: leapR package identifies pathways enriched across diverse 'omics experiments. leverages tabular expression data (proteomics, transcriptomics) using `SummarizedExperiment` object. works pathway .gct file format.

title: leapR: Layered enrichment analysis of pathways R — leapR-package
text: Maintainer: Sara Gosline sara.gosline@pnnl.gov (ORCID) Authors: Jason McDermott jason.mcdermott@pnnl.gov contributors: Vincent Danna vincent.danna@pnnl.gov [contributor] National Institutes Health [funder]

title: leapR — leapR
text: leapR wrapper function consolidates multiple enrichment methods.

title: leapR — leapR
code: leapR(geneset, enrichment_method, eset, assay_name, ...)

title: leapR — leapR
text: geneset list four vectors, gene names, gene descriptions, gene sizes matrix genes. represents .gmt format pathway files. enrichment_method character string specifying method enrichment performed, one : "enrichment_comparison", "enrichment_in_order", "enrichment_in_sets", "enrichment_in_pathway", "correlation_enrichment". eset `SummarizedExperiment` object containing expression data, features rows n sample/conditions columns. assay_name assay analyzed within `eset`. Recommended describe data type (e.g. transcriptomics, proteomics) can integrated `combine_omics` ... arguments

title: leapR — leapR
text: data frame results

title: leapR — leapR
text: arguments enrichment method optional argument information: Enrichment Methods:  enrichment_comparison  Compares distribution abundances two sets conditions pathway using t test. pathway geneset uses t test compare distribution abundance values/numbers eset primary_columns eset secondary_columns. Lower p-values pathways indicate expression pathway significantly different set conditions primary_columns set conditions secondary_columns. Optionally, users can specify fdr calculate empirical p-value randomizing abundances fdr number times. min_p_threshold specified method return pathways adjusted p-value lower specified threshold. sample_n specified method subsample pathway members specified number components.  enrichment_in_order  Calculates enrichment pathways based ranked list using Kolmogorov-Smirnov test. pathway geneset uses Kolmogorov-Smirnov test rank order test distribution ranked abundance values eset primary_columns significant relative random distribution. Note currently primary_columns accepts single column method.  enrichment_in_sets  Calculates enrichment pathway membership list (e.g. highly differential proteins) relative background using Fisher's exact test. pathway geneset uses Fisher's exact test - - representation list components specified. targets specified must vector identifiers serve target list comparison. eset primary_columns specified threshold specifies threshold value determining target list components test. Specifying greaterthan False result components values lower specified threshold. eset data frame matrix, background used calculation taken rownames eset  enrichment_in_pathway  Compares distribution abundances pathway background distribution abundances using t test. pathway geneset calculates significance difference abundances pathway members versus abundance non-pathway members set conditions specified primary_columns. Optionally, users can specify fdr calculate empirical p-value randomizing abundances fdr number times. min_p_threshold specified method return pathways adjusted p-value lower specified threshold. sample_n specified method subsample pathway members specified number components.  correlation_enrichment  Calculates enrichment pathway based correlation pathway members across conditions versus correlation members pathway. pathway geneset calculates pairwise correlation pathway members non-pathway members across specified primary_columns conditions eset. Note large matrices can take long time. p-value calculated based comparing correlation within members pathway correlation values members pathway non-members pathway.

method='libcurl',destfile='transData.rda')  load('transData.rda')  p <- file.remove("transData.rda")   # read in the pathways  data("ncipid")   # read in the patient groups  data("shortlist")  data("longlist")   # use enrichment_comparison to calculate enrichment in one set of  # conditions (shortlist) and another (longlist)  short_v_long = leapR(geneset=ncipid, assay_name='transcriptomics',               enrichment_method='enrichment_comparison',               eset=tset, primary_columns=shortlist,                secondary_columns=longlist)   # use enrichment_in_sets to calculate the most enriched pathways  # from the highest abundance proteins  #     from one condition  onept_sets = leapR(geneset=ncipid, assay_name='transcriptomics',
